name: io.github.cisco-open/network-sketcher
title: Network Sketcher (Local MCP)
description: AI-native MCP server that wraps the Network Sketcher engine to design and manage Cisco network configurations from LLM clients (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.). Generates L1/L2/L3 topology diagrams (PowerPoint and SVG) and manages device data (Excel) entirely from natural-language conversations with the agent.

install.description: Add to Cursor's mcp.json (File > Preferences > Cursor Settings > MCP).
Register with the Claude Code CLI. Use --scope user|project as needed.
Clone the repository, install Python dependencies, then launch the server directly.
install.command: git clone https://github.com/cisco-open/network-sketcher && cd network-sketcher/network-sketcher_local_mcp && python -m pip install -r requirements_mcp.txt && python ns_mcp_server.py

tools.name: get_workspace_info
tools.description: Return the active workspace path and the list of .nsm master files (and other NS-generated files) in it. Always safe to call.

tools.name: suggest_workspace
tools.description: Suggest OS-appropriate (Windows / macOS / Linux) workspace directory candidates under the user's home, with hints about which exist and are writable. Always safe to call.

tools.name: set_workspace
tools.description: Set the active workspace directory for this session. Validates that the path is under the user's home directory, creates it if missing, and confirms it is writable.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: Absolute path (or '~/...') under the user's home directory.

tools.name: create_empty_master
tools.description: Create a new empty Network Sketcher master file (.nsm) in the active workspace.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: Optional target name; must start with '[MASTER]' and end with '.nsm'. Defaults to '[MASTER]no_data.nsm'.

tools.name: get_network_state
tools.description: Run the standard set of show commands and return aggregated results - a lightweight summary of the network state for the agent.

arguments.name: master
arguments.description: Master filename (e.g. '[MASTER]office.nsm') in the active workspace, or an absolute path under it.

tools.name: get_ai_context
tools.description: Generate the full AI Context file for a master and return its contents (network state plus the full CLI command reference). Recommended bootstrap call before issuing any edits.

arguments.name: master
arguments.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

tools.name: run_commands
tools.description: Execute one or more Network Sketcher CLI commands (add / rename / delete / show) against a .nsm master. Multiple lines are batched into a single subprocess to amortise startup cost. Requires get_ai_context to have been called at least once in this session.

arguments.name: master
arguments.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

tools.name: import_master
tools.description: Convert an existing .xlsx master into a .nsm in the active workspace. Use once at the start of a session to bring an existing .xlsx master under .nsm management.

arguments.name: xlsx_path
arguments.description: Absolute path to the source .xlsx file.

arguments.name: target_name
arguments.description: Optional output filename ('[MASTER]*.nsm'). Defaults to the source basename.

tools.name: export_master_xlsx
tools.description: Convert a .nsm master back to .xlsx for use with Excel or the Network Sketcher Offline edition. The .nsm itself is not modified.

arguments.name: master
arguments.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

tools.name: export_diagram
tools.description: Export an L1, L2, or L3 network diagram for the given .nsm master. Output format is SVG (default, fast, browser-renderable) or PPTX (editable in PowerPoint). When 'area' is omitted, the legacy behaviour is preserved (L1: all_areas_tag, L3: all_areas, L2: first area). When 'area' is supplied, only that single area is generated (L1/L3 require format='svg'; L2 supports area for both formats). Requires get_ai_context to have been called at least once in this session.

arguments.name: master
arguments.description: Master filename in the active workspace.

arguments.name: area
arguments.description: Optional area name. When given, only that area's diagram is generated; otherwise the legacy all-areas-or-first-area behaviour applies. For L1/L3 this requires format='svg'.

tools.name: export_device_table_html
tools.description: Export an interactive HTML Device Table preview from a .nsm master (.xlsx masters are rejected). The output is a single self-contained HTML file ('[DEVICE_TABLE]{basename}.html') with six tabs (L1 / L2 / L3 / Attribute / IP Address_Summary / IP Address_List) whose layout matches the Network Sketcher Online edition's Device Preview screen, including sticky-header tables, per-tab CSV/HTML download buttons, and URL-hash initial-tab selection. No external CDN or scripts are referenced, so the file can be opened directly from disk or shared as a single artifact.

arguments.name: master
arguments.description: Master filename in the active workspace (e.g. '[MASTER]office.nsm').

prompts.name: start_ns_session
prompts.description: Bootstrap a Network Sketcher session by surveying co-registered MCP servers, ensuring a workspace is active, and loading the AI context for the given master before any edits.

resources.name: Workspace overview
resources.description: JSON snapshot of the active workspace directory and its inventory of .nsm masters and generated files.

resources.name: CLI command reference
resources.description: Full Network Sketcher CLI command reference (network-sketcher_online/ns_engine/nsm_extensions_cmd_list.txt).
